programme.title: CBC Winnipeg Comedy Festival
programme.desc: Will Sasso hosts a gala night of comedy about the ups and downs of falling in love; featuring Joe Vu, Jen Grant, Amanda Brooke Perrin, and Patrick Haye.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Appa worries about Umma after a minor household accident; Janet uses Jung's past to boost her reputation with students; a high school reunion has Kimchee thinking about an old flame.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Appa makes a controversial call when umpiring pastor Nina's baseball game; Janet gives Umma a sweater that turns out to be a bad fit; Jung and Kimchee babysit Gwen's niece.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Kimchee tries to impress the Handy district manager; Shannon inadvertently insults Umma's soup; Appa starts a prank war with Janet.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: School staff members participate in a complex trust game that will accidentally reveal some of their darkest secrets; Robert gives a trust exercise that fails when he locks himself in a closet.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Culture Day is being celebrated at the academy and Gerry wants the theme Dwyer has already chosen; Leung confesses that he loves Lisa; Callaghan and Trudy come up with a scheme to avoid Callaghan's ex-wife.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry tries to confirm his suspicions that Dwyer is moonlighting as a stripper; Secretary's Day; the staff lottery pool.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: The Critch men are left to their own devices after Mary is hospitalized for a minor procedure; Mark has trouble balancing the household chores and accidentally gives Pops the wrong pills.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Mark is forced to participate in the school's Sports Day; Fox fights for her mother's attention and attempts to break the school record for gold medals.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira go to Roland's Hawaiian party; Alexis and Ted host a dinner party; David and Stevie's relationship heats up.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira's sister Deedee visits; David agrees to talk to a student about being different.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny tries to plan a surprise party for Moira's birthday, but she ends up taking over his plans.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny finds a potential buyer for the town; David and Alexis realize they have become attached to Schitt's Creek.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate learns a lesson about child care; Anne hires a mean nanny; Jenny favours work life to her home life.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate's mother pressures her to cut the leash on her dog; Frankie misreads Anne's advice; Anne's daughter starts to bond with the nanny.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate is shamed for her adult movie preference; Jenny makes a move with Marvin; Frankie searches for the root of her intimacy issues.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate makes a discovery at work; Anne tries to bond with her daughter; Jenny helps Ian with his movie.

programme.desc: School staff members participate in a complex trust game that will accidentally reveal some of their darkest secrets; Robert gives a trust exercise that fails when he locks himself in a closet.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Eleanor tries to help Kate find a new nanny; Jenny organizes a bachelorette party; Frankie and Giselle get some help with their intimacy issue.

programme.title: Strays
programme.desc: Shannon enlists her new neighbor, a reporter, to do a story on the shelter; Paul reluctantly agrees to give Kristian a tattoo.

programme.title: Baroness von Sketch Show
programme.sub-title: There's A Special Place in Hell
programme.desc: No one wants to be a gay cliche; after 40, you're gonna want to exclusively drink white wine; a fierce manicure.

programme.title: CBC Winnipeg Comedy Festival
programme.desc: Howie Miller hosts a gala night of comedy about insecurities, bad habits and personal shortcomings; featuring Julie Kim, Matt Wright, and Kathleen McGee.
